content:
Hi all,

I have been recently learning Rust for Linux and would like to contribute to the upstream. However, after briefly reviewing the current code of Rust for Linux, I did not understand how Rust for Linux adapts to the driver layer. What documents should I search for or study to help me achieve this goal?

What type of driver are you wanting to write? There are Rust bindings for many different driver types in the kernel today (misc, platform, pci, etc.) and more being posted for review every week (led, i2c, etc.)

So perhaps what you are looking for is already present?

Yerkebulan Tulibergenov said:

Have you seen this resource? https://rust-exercises.ferrous-systems.com/latest/book/building-linux-kernel-driver

Well, not yet. I think it's useful for me, I will read this later.

Greg KH said:

What type of driver are you wanting to write? There are Rust bindings for many different driver types in the kernel today (misc, platform, pci, etc.) and more being posted for review every week (led, i2c, etc.)

So perhaps what you are looking for is already present?

humm, in fact, I want to do some simple driver to start my contribute(such as led etc), but in the kernel source, I haven't find any for that. So, maybe should I view the mailing list?

Miao Chen said:

humm, in fact, I want to do some simple driver to start my contribute(such as led etc), but in the kernel source, I haven't find any for that. So, maybe should I view the mailing list?

LED bindings are on the mailing list, yes. See https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20251027200547.1038967-1-markus.probst@posteo.de/ for the latest version of the patches.

Greg KH said:

Miao Chen said:

humm, in fact, I want to do some simple driver to start my contribute(such as led etc), but in the kernel source, I haven't find any for that. So, maybe should I view the mailing list?

LED bindings are on the mailing list, yes. See https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20251027200547.1038967-1-markus.probst@posteo.de/ for the latest version of the patches.

well, thanks for your reply, I'll learn it. May I ask by the way, are there any other simple devices that have not yet worked and require binding?
Greg KH has marked this topic as resolved.
